_comment: Custom Azure RBAC role assigned to the Worker Function's MSI at the management-group scope (NOT subscription wildcard, NOT tenant root). The management group bounds the blast radius to the in-scope subscriptions; the Worker Function cannot escape to another tenant or to subscriptions outside the management-group hierarchy. The DenyProtectedUsers section enumerates the protected ObjectIds and UPN patterns that this skill refuses to touch — mirrored in src/function_worker/handler.py DEFAULT_PROTECTED_UPN_PATTERNS.
Name: iam-departures-azure-entra-worker
Description: Cross-subscription role for the iam-departures-azure-entra Worker Function. Bounded to a management group; cannot escape the tenant.

Permissions._justification: Read + delete role assignments at the bound scope. write/delete on definitions is denied so this role cannot widen its own permissions.

Permissions._explanation: Even though Azure custom-role permissions do not natively support principalId-based deny semantics the way AWS IAM Deny statements do, this section is the authoritative deny list mirrored into the Worker Function's protected_principals check. Any change here MUST also be reflected in src/function_worker/handler.py DEFAULT_PROTECTED_UPN_PATTERNS or scripts/validate_safe_skill_bar.py will fail the build. The runtime guard is the actual enforcer; this section documents the operator's contract for what the role must never do.

_deployment_notes.method: Deploy via az role definition create + az role assignment create at the management group scope from the security OU subscription.
_deployment_notes.command: az role definition create --role-definition cross_subscription_role.json && az role assignment create --assignee ${WorkerMsiObjectId} --role iam-departures-azure-entra-worker --scope /providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/${ManagementGroupId}
